INTRODUCTION
In recent years, we could observe an increased research interest
in session-based recommendation problems.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
In such settings, the
problem is not to make relevance predictions for items given the
users’ long-term preferences, but to make recommendations given
only a few user interactions in an ongoing session [19].
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
However, recent research [7, 15] indicates that simpler
methods based on nearest-neighbor techniques can outperform
gru4rec in terms of certain accuracy measures.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting
concepts.open_question:
How to address the limitation described: However, recent research [7, 15] indicates that simpler
methods based on nearest-neighbor techniques can outperform
gru4rec in terms of certain accuracy measures.

concepts.finding:
Overall, across the domains
we can observe that only in one single case—when using the RSC15
dataset—a rather early complex model was able to outperform
relatively simple baselines.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
At the same time, some
algorithms can focus their recommendations on a small set of items
that are recommended to everyone, which can be undesired in
certain domains and lead to limited personalization.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Overall, we hope that this environment is help-
ful for other researchers to achieve higher levels of reproducibility
and faster progress in this area.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In this work, we focus on the fast-developing
area of session-based recommendation and aim to contribute to a
better understanding of what represents the state-of-the-art.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The evaluation of the algorithms under
the exact same conditions revealed that the benefits of applying
today’s neural approaches to session-based recommendations are
still limited.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
